name: Kloner 2003 Tadalafil Cardiovascular Effects Extract
population_or_context: More than 4000 subjects receiving tadalafil in clinical pharmacology, phase 2, phase 3, and open-label studies, including healthy subjects, men with mild ED, and cardiovascular-disease subgroups.
methods: Pooled safety and cardiovascular-effects analysis reporting blood-pressure changes in healthy subjects and mild ED, nitrate coadministration hemodynamics, myocardial infarction incidence, stroke incidence, and cardiovascular safety interpretation.
population_profile: Mixed tadalafil clinical-development population including healthy men and ED/cardiovascular disease groups; partly direct for healthy male BP effects but dated and not an off-label outcome trial.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil exposure across clinical pharmacology and ED studies, including daily tadalafil 20 mg for 26 weeks in healthy male subjects or mild ED.
indication_context: Clinical pharmacology and ED development safety evidence.
study_design: Pooled safety analysis and cardiovascular-effects review of clinical trials and open-label studies.
comparator_context: Placebo and expected population rates depending on analysis.
safety_findings: Nitrate coadministration produced clinically important BP lowering in some contexts; tadalafil remained contraindicated with nitrates.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate for BP effects in healthy male subjects, low for off-label cardiovascular benefit because outcomes were safety surveillance rather than prevention efficacy.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Older pooled analysis, clinical-development populations, selected trial eligibility, event-rate comparison limitations, and abstract-level extraction.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is safety-pool design and lack of clinical cardiovascular benefit endpoint.
